sourceMovie.title: Dave
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Dave (1993) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Ivan Reitman's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Forrest Gump
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dave clicked for you, Forrest Gump (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dave clicked for you, Love Actually (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dave?
faq.answer: Try Forrest Gump, Love Actually, The Man from U.N.C.L.E.—each shares DNA with Dave while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dave?
faq.answer: Dave (1993) is best described as Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dave worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
